title: season — compliance evidence
description: Season is a calendrical + commercial container. No PII on the row itself. The bulk of compliance responsibility is around FINANCIAL-record retention (late_registration_fee_cents changes generate 7-year audit rows) + SOC 2 CC6.3 change tracking (SeasonCurrentChanged is audit-critical because year-over-year reports pivot on the current-season flag). Minor-safeguarding regulator interest applies to date-shift events on programs serving under-18s (families need advance notice of schedule changes).

controls.evidence: Season fields are used only for calendrical scoping (start / end / registration / playoff dates) + entitlement enforcement + reporting frame. Never resold, never joined with third-party enrichment.
Archived Seasons retained 2555 days (7 years — financial-record retention floor). Financial audit rows outlive their source Season per that floor. Retention lives in `retention.json`.

Every Season mutation is `Auditable`; every status transition + is_current flip + date shift + sport_key change writes to `audits` with actor, timestamp, changed_fields, request_id, before + after snapshots (for financial-relevant fields). 7-year retention for financial-record entries.

Every write path is behind SeasonPolicy. Lifecycle transitions (open-registration, start, complete, archive) each carry a distinct permission. `seasons.set_current` is owner-only because current-season changes shift YoY reporting frames — narrowest privilege in the module.

controls.principle: authorisation of changes to system components
controls.evidence: SeasonCurrentChanged is the top audit-critical event — every flip writes to `audits` with 7-year retention + fires a cannot-opt-out notification to owner + admin. SeasonDatesShifted post-in_progress writes with warn severity. SeasonSportKeyChanged writes with critical severity + is refused outright post-in_progress.

Health probes cover FK integrity + current-season invariant + date consistency + slug uniqueness + phase-roll heartbeat + purge queue drain. The `CurrentSeasonInvariantProbe` catches drift the observer normally prevents; failure pages ops.

Season.start_date + end_date changes on COMPLETED Seasons are refused outright (SEASON_DATES_LOCKED_AFTER_COMPLETION — 409). Historical Invoices attribute to a season's fiscal period; retroactive edits would corrupt reconciliation. The `seasons.dates.shift` permission (owner-only) permits in-progress shifts but every shift writes to `audits` with 7-year retention + fires SeasonDatesShifted (cannot-opt-out notification to enrolled athletes).

Season.late_registration_fee_cents changes generate `financial-record` classified audit rows retained 7 years. The `regulated_financial` field classification in `data-classes.json` documents this explicitly.

controls.provision: advance-notice for schedule changes on minors' programs
controls.evidence: SeasonDatesShifted fires a cannot-opt-out `season.dates_shifted` notification to enrolled athletes + coaches. When the enrolled athletes are minors (Wave 3 athlete-enrollment carries the age-band), the notification additionally CC's the athlete's guardian per the Wave 3 guardian-relationships module. Regulators (US state childcare regs, UK Ofsted) treat sudden schedule changes on minors' programs as an audit event.

SeasonSportKeyChanged is refused post-in_progress (SEASON_SPORT_KEY_LOCKED_AFTER_START — 409, critical severity). A mid-season sport shift would drift downstream Team + Enrollment references — for a football program that suddenly becomes basketball, the safeguarding-adjacent risk assessments (coach ratios, equipment safety) would silently become invalid.

Aliased to the GDPR Art. 17 erasure flow. Season rows themselves are not PII; the cascade on TenantErased cleans them up when the tenant is erased. Individual athlete deletion requests are handled by the Wave 3 athlete-enrollment module — those deletions leave the Season row intact but remove the athlete's enrollment.
